description: Shared Weft conformance vector for the unsigned Wardline->legis dirty dev-artifact path. Wardline emits this shape when scan --format legis --allow-dirty is used on a dirty working tree; Legis consumes the same dirty key to distinguish keyless dev, CI skip, and explicit dev-mode governance. The artifact is intentionally unsigned because signing dirty working-tree content would assert false tree provenance.

signing.policy: dirty dev artifacts are never signed; a configured consumer key without dev-mode must return SKIPPED_DIRTY_TREE

valid.name: dirty_unsigned_dev_artifact
valid.description: Unsigned dirty-tree dev artifact: dirty must be strict boolean true, artifact_signature must be absent, and findings remains present even when empty.
